content.claim: Hungary tracks students earlier than OECD average—partially at Grade 5 (age 10-11) or Grade 7 (age 12-13)—with approximately 85% of Hungarian students in schools that select by academic performance, compared to 43% OECD average
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Cross-national comparisons do not control for differences in curriculum intensity or labor-market demand
Percentage enrolled does not measure intensity or consequence of selection
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
